tactical_shape: Struggled to maximise width for large portions of the first half
Switched Jérémy Doku to the right after Egypt doubled up on him
Used Romelu Lukaku as a super sub from the bench in the 66th minute
standout_players: Romelu Lukaku made an instant impact off the bench, drawing defenders and forcing the equalising own goal
Jérémy Doku was heavily targeted and often doubled up on by Egypt
Leandro Trossard was indecisive in possession and lost the ball three times in the opening half-hour
Kevin De Bruyne attempted speculative long-range efforts
fatigue_injuries: The article notes Lukaku has only logged 40 minutes in Serie A for Napoli this season, raising concerns about readiness
Garcia said Belgium must go easy on Lukaku so he does not get hurt
home_read.momentum: Rescued a point from a difficult first-half position, with Lukaku’s bench role looking like a useful weapon going forward.

tactical_shape: Proactive pressing when Belgium tried to build down the wings
Fell into a more composed mid-block/possession approach after taking the lead, with the defence well stationed behind
Broke on the counter when able
standout_players: Mohamed Salah created the opener with a sharp pass from the right
Emam Ashour scored Egypt’s goal with a shot underneath Thomas Meunier
Egypt effectively doubled up on Jérémy Doku and limited Belgium's width
away_read.momentum: A credible opening display against a presumptive frontrunner, though they let the lead slip after Belgium introduced Lukaku.
